title: Chinese Chicken Soup (Community Enhanced)
original_instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add chile paste, ginger, and turmeric; cook and stir until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes.
Stir in chicken broth, chicken, celery, soy sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil; add noodles and simmer for 3 minutes.
Stir in lettuce and remove from heat. Garnish servings with green onions.
ingredients: 1 cup chopped carrots and yellow onion

line_diffs.reasoning: Carrots and yellow onion add more flavor and texture than celery. Cabbage provides a different texture and flavor profile compared to lettuce. Sriracha sauce is a suitable alternative to red chili paste. Shredded cabbage holds its crunch better in hot soup than lettuce. Using sweet chile sauce instead of chile paste adds a different flavor profile. Experimenting with different noodle types like spaghetti can provide a new twist to the soup. Substituting spinach for lettuce provides a different texture and flavor profile.

source_review.text: This soup is really good. I was skeptical about the lettuce, it didn't make a lot of sense to me to put cold lettuce in hot soup, so I used spinach like other reviewers and it was actually really good. Along with celery I added mushrooms and yellow onions. Upon reading a review that the celery was too crunchy, I cooked all the vegtables with the oil and spices, before adding the chicken or the chicken broth so they wouldn't be so crunchy. Also, I have never had luck finding chile paste at the grocery store, so I substituted it with sweet chile sauce and it worked great. I used ramen noodles but might try spaghetti noodles next time. This is a nice recipe to make if you have extra vegetables you don't know what to do with, as this recipe could be made using a lot more vegetables than it calls for. It smells good and tastes great.

description: This easy Chinese chicken soup with noodles is flavored with turmeric, chile paste, ginger, and soy for a quick and flavorsome meal in one pot.
